briefing.headline: Rig up a Walking Topwater — aggressive fall approach for stained water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Walking Topwater is a strong choice for fall with stained water and pre frontal conditions.

Wheeler: Uses a constant steady retrieve twitching with a little slack; when a fish blows up and misses, continue working the bait - slow it down but snap it harder to make it look injured but still running away from the fish.

Key adjustment for pre frontal: aggressive, faster retrieves — fish are feeding actively before the front.

primaryApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: Uses a constant steady retrieve twitching with a little slack; when a fish blows up and misses, continue working the bait - slow it down but snap it harder to make it look injured but still r
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on flat, creek channel, grass. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Robertson: When a deep pattern dies due to wind shift muddying the water, switch to a modified walking topwater (Sexy Dawg with SuspenDots and upgraded treble hooks to reduce buoyancy) targeting bass
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on flat, creek channel, grass. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: Robertson: Look for bass hunting bait in small feeding groups called 'wolf packs' — these aggressive groups will respond well to topwater presentations in pockets and around scattered rock.
Wheeler: In fall, fish school up in ditches, on little flats in 2-4 feet, and especially around grass; on grassless lakes, key on shallow ditches with stumps that let bass ambush shad.

KVD: Clear water on grass lakes = fish can see the bait from farther away (benefit) but can also be spooky (challenge). Fish may be timid — bites are subtle, just bumping the bait. Slow down and keep working the area.

briefing.depthStrategy: Pre-frontal fall bass are following shad into the backs of creeks. Fish can be anywhere from 3-18ft depending on bait position. Target creek arms, secondary points, and anywhere baitfish are concentrating.
adjustIf: If the front stalls and conditions stabilize, dial back aggression and focus on higher-percentage structure
If water clears significantly, downsize presentations and move to natural color patterns
If fish are blowing up but not connecting, pause 3-5 seconds after each strike before resuming
